description: File I/O stabilization, QThreadPool worker patterns, watchdog observer lifecycle, and thread-safety for PySide6 signals.

pseudocode: if size == prev_size and mtime == prev_mtime:
if stable_count >= STABLE_COUNT_REQUIRED: emit file_ready(path); break
edge_cases: Skip files that are still open by another process (try opening with exclusive lock; on Windows use msvcrt.locking).
Ignore temporary files: skip paths ending in .tmp, .part, ~.
Handle zero-byte files: require size > 0 before starting stable count.

watchdog_lifecycle.observer_thread: watchdog.observers.Observer runs in its own daemon thread.
watchdog_lifecycle.event_handler: Subclass FileSystemEventHandler; override on_created and on_moved.
watchdog_lifecycle.multi_folder: Call observer.schedule() multiple times with different paths before start().
watchdog_lifecycle.note: watchdog events arrive on the Observer thread — do NOT call Qt widgets from there. Instead push to a thread-safe queue.Queue and dispatch from the main thread via QTimer.singleShot(0, process_queue).

base_worker.signals_class: Must be a separate QObject subclass (QRunnable is not a QObject).
pattern: self.fn = fn; self.args = args; self.kwargs = kwargs

signals_thread_safety.rule: Signals connected across threads are automatically queued by Qt (Qt.QueuedConnection). Never call UI methods directly from a background thread.
signals_thread_safety.explicit_queue: Use Signal.emit() — Qt routes delivery to the receiver's thread.
signals_thread_safety.mutex_use: Use QMutex or threading.Lock for shared mutable state (e.g., processing set to prevent duplicate OCR jobs).

processing_guard.description: Track in-flight file paths to prevent duplicate jobs.

memory_management.thumbnail_cache: Use functools.lru_cache or a bounded dict (max 200 entries) keyed by (path, mtime) to avoid holding stale pixmaps.
memory_management.pixmap_lifecycle: QPixmap must be created on the main thread. Workers produce QImage (thread-safe) and emit it; main thread converts to QPixmap.
memory_management.pdf_handles: Always call fitz.Document.close() in a try/finally block to release file handles immediately.
